summary: Iter14 corrections (Spanish UI) — backend + frontend smoke. Ran full pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/ → 229 passed, 1 skipped (matches expected). All 9 new iter14 tests in test_iter14_corrections.py pass (normal user can withdraw, employee cannot, confirmed-order lock, paid-withdrawal lock, payout_proof_image required for transfer, allowed_currencies filtering). Playwright smoke against deployed REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL verified the 5 user-requested UI behaviors: (1) /admin/users — Saldo cell is read-only ('50 USD' text, no input), allowed-currencies-row + OK button present for employee row; (2) admin dashboard sidebar does NOT contain nav-vip; (3) normal user sidebar shows nav-vip with label 'Saldo y Retiros'; (4) /admin/withdrawals — status badges use Spanish 'Confirmado / Pagado / Pendiente / Rechazado' (no 'APPROVED'/'PAID' leakage), dialog has withdrawal-approve='Confirmar', withdrawal-pay='Pagado', withdrawal-reject + payout-proof-input file field (payout-tx-hash correctly hidden for transfer method); (5) /admin/orders dialog approve-order button text is 'Confirmar' and status column shows lowercase 'confirmado'. VipView header renders '/ Saldo y Retiros' for normal users. ONE backend regression vs iter14 spec found: GET /api/vip/balances still returns 403 for role=normal even though iter14 enables them to accumulate balance — causes a visible React error overlay on /dashboard/vip for normal users.

minor.issue: Returns 403 for role=normal (server.py L1147 still gates on role in ('vip','admin')). Iter14 spec says normal users can accumulate balance, so this guard now contradicts the feature. Causes axios 403 → React dev error overlay on /dashboard/vip for normal users even though page content renders. Fix is one-line: include 'normal' in the allowlist. Also requires updating legacy test tests/test_multicurrency_and_stats.py::TestVipBalancesEndpoint::test_normal_403 (currently asserts 403 → flip to 200).

ui_bugs.issue: React dev error overlay 'Uncaught runtime errors: Request failed with status code 403' shown on load because /api/vip/balances returns 403. Page itself renders OK ('Sin retiros aún', 'Aún no tienes saldo acumulado') but UX is broken by the overlay. Root cause is the backend guard above.

integration_issues.flow: Normal user view of 'Saldo y Retiros'
integration_issues.issue: Promise.all in VipView.jsx (axios.get(`${API}/vip/balances`)) rejects for normal users, surfacing as unhandled error. Either relax the backend guard OR wrap the balances call in its own try/catch with .catch(()=>setBalances({balances:[],total_usdt:0})) like Marketplace/OverviewView already do.

issues: React warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' — accessibility hygiene, add DialogDescription to each.

action_items: Backend: relax /api/vip/balances guard to include role 'normal' (server.py L1147) so iter14's 'normal can accumulate balance' feature is fully usable; then update tests/test_multicurrency_and_stats.py::TestVipBalancesEndpoint::test_normal_403 to expect 200 (or rename to test_normal_200).

Frontend: as a defensive fallback, wrap the /vip/balances axios call in VipView.jsx with its own .catch so a 403 does not surface as an Uncaught runtime error overlay (matches the pattern used in OverviewView.jsx).
Frontend: add DialogDescription (or aria-describedby) to AdminWithdrawals and AdminOrders manage-dialogs to clear the React a11y warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is still ~2200 lines — past the 700-line guideline. Recommend extracting routers (admin_orders, admin_withdrawals, vip, totp, marketplace, audit) into /app/backend/routers/* in a future refactor.

Role guards are scattered as inline `if user['role'] not in (...)` checks across many endpoints. Centralising via a `require_roles(*allowed)` dependency would have caught the /vip/balances regression noticed in this iteration.

Both AdminWithdrawals.jsx and VipView.jsx duplicate the status-label maps (cash → Entregado/En progreso, transfer/crypto → Pagado/Confirmado). Extract to a single helper in /app/frontend/src/lib/withdrawalStatus.js to honour DRY.

TotpPromptDialog is imported into AdminUsers/AdminWithdrawals/AdminOrders/VipView — good reuse. Confirm it always strips the TOTP code from state after submit so it is not held in memory longer than needed.

success_rate.frontend: 5 of 5 iter14 user-requested UI behaviors verified; 1 secondary regression discovered (/vip/balances 403 → React error overlay for normal users)

test_credentials: Used session-cookie injection of test_session_admin_X and test_session_normal_X (per /app/memory/test_credentials.md and conftest.py). TOTP secret JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXPJBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP seeded on admin/employee/vip/normal by conftest helpers.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. pytest conftest auto-enables TOTP idempotently and a single normal-user balance row is set + cleaned up by test_iter14_corrections.py.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter14 (Spanish UI) is feature-complete on the backend and the 5 user-requested UI behaviors all verified via Playwright with cookie injection. The only outstanding gap is GET /api/vip/balances still 403'ing for normal users — this is a one-line server.py fix plus a stale test in test_multicurrency_and_stats.py::test_normal_403 that needs to flip from 403→200. After that, re-run /dashboard/vip with token test_session_normal_X and confirm there is no 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay. Cookie injection pattern that worked: `await page.context.add_cookies([{name:'session_token', value:'test_session_X_X', url:'<REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL>', secure:True, sameSite:'Lax'}])`.
rca of the issue: Iter14 expanded normal users' capabilities to accumulate balance and request withdrawals. POST /api/vip/withdraw and GET /api/vip/withdrawals/mine were correctly relaxed (the former opens to non-employees, the latter uses require_user with no role gate). However GET /api/vip/balances still enforces `user['role'] not in ('vip','admin') → 403`, so a normal user with accumulated balance cannot see it. Because VipView.jsx fires /vip/balances + /vip/withdrawals/mine in Promise.all without per-call .catch, the rejected balances promise surfaces as a React Uncaught runtime error overlay even though the rest of the page degrades to the empty state. Mitigation: (a) include 'normal' in the role allowlist on server.py L1147 and update the legacy test_normal_403 assertion; (b) optionally harden VipView to swallow that 403 locally.
